message:
feat(conformance): handle deferred blocking replies in multi-client diff

crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs
  * `run_live_redis_multi_client_diff`: upgrades the
    `pending_runtime_responses` map value from a raw
    `(u64, RespFrame)` pair to a new local `enum
    PendingRuntimeResponse { Immediate(RespFrame),
    Deferred(RespFrame) }` tag so the harness can distinguish two
    runtime outcomes at the `send_only` step:
      - `Immediate`: runtime already has the authoritative reply;
        compare it when `read_pending` arrives.
      - `Deferred`: runtime returned `RespFrame::Array(None)` — the
        null-array placeholder that represents "would have blocked
        on the real server." In this case we store the original
        request frame and re-run the command on the later
        `read_pending` step, so the comparison is against the
        eventual wake-up reply instead of the immediate null.
  * Wiring: the `send_only` branch now matches on
    `matches!(runtime_actual, RespFrame::Array(None))` to pick
    Deferred; the `read_pending` branch demultiplexes the two
    variants, re-executing deferred frames before diffing against
    the oracle.

crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_blocking_multi_client.json
  * Updates six of the existing BLPOP/BRPOP/BLMOVE/XREAD-BLOCK step
    envelopes to reflect the new deferred-vs-immediate distinction
    in the goldens. No new cases added in this commit — just
    matches the updated harness semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
